caution.use_note: Inspect wet/dry boundaries, shallow transition areas, local peak-depth areas, and pixel-level risk maps.

high-risk.action: do not use alone for warning decisions
high-risk.use_note: Trigger conservative interpretation, hydrodynamic-model confirmation, or expert review before warning decisions.

reliable.use_note: Prediction is within the stronger applicability range of the current surrogate model.

applicability_boundary_rows.caution_note: Warning labels are operational interpretation labels derived from deterministic Phase 15 screening outputs. They are not calibrated probabilities, Bayesian uncertainty estimates, or formal confidence intervals.
applicability_boundary_rows.condition_description: Scenarios with Phase 15 reliable labels and no strong risk components.
applicability_boundary_rows.recommended_use: Use as a rapid flood-depth reference with normal review.

applicability_boundary_rows.caution_note: Wet/dry boundaries are sensitive to small depth and threshold changes.
applicability_boundary_rows.condition_description: Cells near predicted or target wet/dry transitions.
applicability_boundary_rows.evidence_source: Phase 15 boundary false-dry and pixel boundary fractions
applicability_boundary_rows.recommended_use: Inspect with pixel risk maps and avoid overinterpreting exact edge placement.

applicability_boundary_rows.caution_note: These labels are screening indicators, not calibrated confidence intervals.
applicability_boundary_rows.condition_description: Cells near the wet-depth threshold or low confidence-margin band.
applicability_boundary_rows.recommended_use: Use with targeted review of shallow transition areas.

applicability_boundary_rows.caution_note: This is an applicability-boundary warning for the current surrogate model.
applicability_boundary_rows.evidence_source: Phase 15 high_intensity_reason and Phase 13 consistency check
applicability_boundary_rows.recommended_use: Do not use prediction alone; trigger hydrodynamic-model or expert confirmation.

applicability_boundary_rows.caution_note: Peak-depth review is required where local maxima control warning severity.
applicability_boundary_rows.condition_description: Cases or pixels with peak-depth underprediction or deep-depth underprediction signals.
applicability_boundary_rows.recommended_use: Check possible local-depth underprediction before warning decisions.

applicability_boundary_rows.caution_note: Repeated false-dry labels indicate spatial screening risk, not event probability.
applicability_boundary_rows.condition_description: Pixels repeatedly dry in prediction when wet in target across Phase 15 screening artifacts.
applicability_boundary_rows.recommended_use: Treat as missed-inundation caution zones in spatial warning interpretation.

applicability_boundary_rows.caution_note: Contraction can indicate extent underestimation in rapid surrogate predictions.
applicability_boundary_rows.condition_description: Scenarios where predicted inundation extent is substantially smaller than target extent.
applicability_boundary_rows.recommended_use: Review inundation extent before operational warning use.

interpretation_note: Warning labels are operational interpretation labels derived from deterministic Phase 15 screening outputs. They are not calibrated probabilities, Bayesian uncertainty estimates, or formal confidence intervals.
limitations: Warning labels are operational interpretation labels derived from deterministic Phase 15 screening outputs. They are not calibrated probabilities, Bayesian uncertainty estimates, or formal confidence intervals.
The rules convert Phase 15 screening labels into operational guidance; they do not replace hydrodynamic simulation or expert judgment in high-stakes final warning decisions.
Pixel warning labels summarize repeated screening signals and should be interpreted as spatial caution guidance.
phase: Phase 16 Reliability-Aware Warning Rules and Applicability Boundary
